name: Weekly Content Engine Operator
summary: A recurring content pipeline that turns raw ideas into publish-ready drafts and a weekly release queue.
problem: Publishing cadence was inconsistent because ideation, drafting, and review lived across disconnected tools.
workflow: Collect topic ideas from notes, meetings, and customer questions.
Cluster ideas by intent and priority for the week.
Generate first drafts in predefined post formats.
Run link/claim checks and hand draft to human editor.
Schedule approved posts and create a distribution checklist.
openclawRole: Generates publishing checklist and reminder prompts.
humanReview: Priority decisions on what ships this week.
output: A reliable weekly publishing batch with fewer missed deadlines and clearer editorial throughput.
lessons: Consistency improved more from process clarity than model sophistication.
A strict editorial checklist prevented quality drift.
One owner for approvals reduced bottlenecks significantly.

name: Competitor Research Briefing Agent
summary: A recurring intelligence workflow that produces weekly competitor movement briefs for decision-making.
problem: Competitive intelligence was ad hoc, scattered, and too slow to influence active roadmap decisions.
workflow: Track a fixed list of competitor sources and change signals.
Collect and normalize updates into one structured brief format.
Highlight significant deltas from prior week baselines.
Draft implications and candidate response actions.
Route brief to decision owners for review and prioritization.
openclawRole: Flags unusual movement requiring manual inspection.
humanReview: Add non-public context before stakeholder distribution.
output: A weekly briefing cadence that improves reaction time without drowning teams in noisy updates.
lessons: Template consistency matters more than breadth of sources.
Human context turns signals into strategy.
Trend comparisons improved confidence in prioritization.

name: Lead Triage and Routing Assistant
summary: A lead intake operator that classifies inbound requests and prepares context-rich follow-up queues.
problem: Leads were being answered inconsistently, with slow response times and poor qualification context.
workflow: Capture incoming leads from forms and inbox channels.
Score lead quality using predefined qualification rules.
Generate concise lead briefs with recommended next action.
Route qualified leads to priority queue with SLA tags.
Create follow-up reminders for stale or pending leads.
openclawRole: Schedules follow-up reminders and queue updates.
humanReview: Custom response drafting for strategic leads.
Policy checks for edge-case inbound messages.
output: Faster first responses, cleaner pipeline visibility, and less lead leakage between inboxes.
lessons: Clear scoring rules prevented random triage decisions.
SLA tags dramatically improved follow-up discipline.
High-value lead review should always stay human-owned.

name: Recurring KPI Report Generator
summary: A weekly reporting agent that prepares stakeholder-ready summaries from operational data.
problem: Weekly reporting consumed too much senior time and often shipped late or with inconsistent formatting.
workflow: Pull KPI snapshots from defined sources.
Normalize numbers and compare against prior periods.
Draft executive summary with notable changes.
Highlight anomalies and unresolved data quality flags.
Publish report draft for owner approval.
openclawRole: Generates draft narrative for trend shifts.
Prepares standardized output format for stakeholders.
output: Predictable reporting cadence with less manual overhead and clearer insight communication.
lessons: Automated drafts are strong, but anomaly interpretation must be human.
Stakeholder trust improved with consistent report structure.

name: Knowledge Base Q&A Assistant
summary: A content-grounded assistant that answers repeated internal questions from approved documentation.
problem: Teams repeatedly asked the same process questions, and answers varied by who responded.
workflow: Ingest approved documentation and SOP references.
Map questions to relevant sections and return cited answers.
Flag gaps where documentation is outdated or missing.
Route unresolved questions to designated owners.
Log unanswered patterns for documentation updates.
openclawRole: Surfaces source links and confidence context.
humanReview: Resolve ambiguous questions with business context.
output: Faster internal response times and more consistent answers across recurring operational questions.
lessons: Answer quality depends on documentation quality.
Citations increase trust and reduce misunderstandings.
Question logs are a powerful roadmap for SOP improvements.
